questions.text: What is a record in computing?

options.text: A list of values all of the same data type
A collection of related fields about a single entity, each with potentially different data types
A file stored on a hard drive
A log of all user actions on a computer

questions.explanation: A record groups related data fields about a single entity. Unlike an array, fields in a record can have different data types (e.g. string, integer, boolean).

questions.text: A 3×4 2D array has how many elements?
questions.explanation: A 3×4 2D array has 3 rows and 4 columns. Total elements = 3 × 4 = 12.

questions.text: How would you access the element at row 2, column 0 of a 2D array named 'grid'?

options.text: grid(2, 0)
grid[0][2]
grid[2][0]
grid{2}{0}

questions.explanation: 2D arrays in WJEC pseudocode are accessed as array[row][col]. So row 2, column 0 is grid[2][0].

questions.text: What is the role of a sensor in an automated system?

options.text: To perform physical actions such as opening a valve
To process data and decide what action to take
To collect data from the physical environment
To display information to a human operator

questions.explanation: Sensors collect (measure) data from the physical environment and convert it to a digital signal for the controller to process. Examples: temperature, motion, light sensors.

options.text: A device that collects environmental data
A device that performs physical actions in response to control signals
Software that controls an automated system
The human-machine interface

questions.explanation: Actuators carry out physical actions (e.g. opening a valve, turning on a motor, triggering an alarm) in response to commands from the controller.

questions.text: In a greenhouse automated control system, what role does the temperature sensor play?

options.text: It opens and closes the vents
It processes temperature data and decides actions
It collects temperature data from the environment
It provides a display for the gardener

questions.explanation: The temperature sensor collects (measures) data from the greenhouse environment. This is the data acquisition function. The controller then processes this to decide whether to open vents or turn on heaters.

questions.text: What is the purpose of feedback in an automated system?

options.text: To allow the human operator to override the system
To allow the controller to check whether its actions achieved the desired result
To log errors for maintenance purposes
To reduce the processing load on the controller

questions.explanation: Feedback closes the loop — after an actuator acts, sensors measure the new state and report back. The controller can then determine whether another action is needed.

questions.text: Which concern does algorithmic bias in AI systems raise?

options.text: AI systems use too much electricity
AI may produce unfair or discriminatory results due to biased training data
AI systems are too slow for real-time applications
AI cannot be programmed using high-level languages

questions.explanation: Algorithmic bias occurs when an AI trained on biased data makes unfair decisions — e.g. a hiring algorithm less likely to recommend certain groups, or facial recognition less accurate for some ethnicities.

questions.text: When would a 1D array be more appropriate than a record?

options.text: When storing details about a single student (name, age, grade)
When storing a list of exam scores all of the same data type
When storing a timetable (days × periods)
When storing product information with mixed fields

questions.explanation: A 1D array is ideal for a list of values of the same type, like a set of scores. Records are better for mixed-type fields about a single entity; 2D arrays for grid/table data.

questions.text: Which legislation must automated systems that process personal data comply with?

options.text: Computer Misuse Act 1990
Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988
Data Protection Act 2018 / UK GDPR
Digital Economy Act 2017

questions.explanation: The Data Protection Act 2018 / UK GDPR requires organisations to protect personal data, use it only for stated purposes, and respect individuals' rights over their data.
